---
title: "ALIGN Drug Pipeline Documentation"
subtitle: "Technical documentation for the health-product integration pipeline"
description: "How ALIGN ingests, resolves, enriches, and publishes health-product evidence."
page-layout: full
---

The ALIGN Drug Pipeline builds a canonical health-product registry by combining product identity, essential-medicines policy, national regulatory authorization, WHO prequalification, clinical evidence, innovation-pipeline membership, and FDA enrichment.

::: {.callout-note}
This site contains stable technical documentation. Exploratory analyses and source-extraction notebooks remain in `reports/` until they are portable and reproducible in an automated documentation build.
:::

## Documentation map

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [Pipeline](pipeline/index.qmd)

Understand the processing sequence, resolution logic, stage outputs, and the figures proposed for documenting each master-record version.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [Data sources](sources/index.qmd)

Review source provenance, capture methods, metadata, and the role of each upstream dataset.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [Integration and matching](integration/index.qmd)

See how source records are resolved against canonical products and how match provenance is retained.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [Data model](data-model/index.qmd)

Learn how nested master records become relational tables and dashboard-facing analytical views.
:::

:::

## Repository conventions

- `docs/` contains reviewed documentation source.
- `reports/` contains executable analyses, extraction notebooks, and works in progress.
- `scripts/` contains ingestion, integration, and enrichment code.
- `data_model/` contains schema definitions, relational transforms, and analytical views.
- `data/` contains local pipeline inputs and outputs and is not treated as documentation source.
